description: Missing broad-scope annotation requirement for ** globs
counterevidenceSought: Review existing permissions-matrix instances to verify whether ** globs are currently used without annotation
alternativeExplanation: Broad globs may be intentionally allowed for legitimate repo-wide patterns; the security risk may be mitigated by operational review rather than schema enforcement
downgradeTrigger: If operational review confirms all ** usage is audited and documented, downgrade to P2

description: Missing deny-precedence semantic check in schema
counterevidenceSought: Verify that runtime specificity ordering in permissions-gate.ts correctly handles deny/allow precedence
alternativeExplanation: Specificity-based ordering may be sufficient if deny rules are always authored with higher specificity than allow rules
downgradeTrigger: If runtime implementation guarantees deny-precedence through specificity or explicit checks, downgrade to P2

counterevidenceSought: Review whether runtime path normalization in permissions-gate.ts mitigates absolute path risks
alternativeExplanation: Runtime path resolution may normalize absolute paths relative to repo root, reducing the risk
downgradeTrigger: If runtime path normalization effectively constrains absolute paths to repo scope, downgrade to P2

description: Missing explicit validation for undefined quota_pool fields
counterevidenceSought: Verify whether TypeScript type system enforces quota_pool presence at compile time
alternativeExplanation: The type definition marks quota_pool as required, so undefined values should be caught at compile time
downgradeTrigger: If TypeScript strict null checks guarantee quota_pool is never undefined at runtime, downgrade to P2

description: Error message leaks full path for iteration_file_missing
counterevidenceSought: Determine whether these error messages are user-facing or internal-only logs
alternativeExplanation: If these errors are only logged internally and never exposed to end users, the security impact is reduced
downgradeTrigger: If verified these errors are internal-only and never exposed to users, downgrade to P2

description: Unknown model name behavior not documented
counterevidenceSought: Verify whether runtime budget lookup code has explicit unknown-model handling
alternativeExplanation: This may be intentional — the reference is advisory until a runtime prompt-pack builder consumes the JSON
downgradeTrigger: If runtime implementation explicitly handles unknown models with a clear error path, downgrade to deferred

summary: Security dimension identifies 9 P1 findings and 1 P2 advisory across schema validation, input validation, and error-message leakage. Core default-deny semantics in permissions-gate.ts are correctly implemented, and backward-compat defaults in agent-config recipes are correct. However, permissions-matrix schema lacks explicit broad-scope and deny-precedence guards, fallback-router does not explicitly validate quota_pool is defined, and post-dispatch-validate leaks filesystem paths in error messages.
